source_item.title: US less reliable? India may need to open the door wider to the Dragon

slide1_hook.label: Modi's own adviser: let China in more
slide1_hook.subhead: Rakesh Mohan pushes India to rethink its China wall

slide2_backstory.label: THE BACKSTORY
slide2_backstory.headline: India shut its China door in 2020
slide2_backstory.body: After the 2020 border clashes, India tightened rules on Chinese investment and stayed out of the RCEP trade deal. India now imports a lot from China but exports very little back, and that gap has stayed wide.

slide3_news.label: THE UPDATE
slide3_news.headline: PMEAC's Rakesh Mohan urges Chinese investment push
slide3_news.body: Rakesh Mohan, a top adviser to PM Modi, wants India to ease curbs on Chinese investment in textiles and footwear. He also wants India to rethink joining RCEP and to seek membership in the CPTPP.

slide4_why.label: WHY IT MATTERS
slide4_why.headline: A potential shift in India's China strategy
bullets: Shows growing support to ease China investment rules
Could bring new investment and jobs in manufacturing
A hedge against unpredictable US trade policy

slide5_engage.label: YOUR TAKE
slide5_engage.headline: Should India ease its China investment rules to boost manufacturing, or is that too risky after 2020?
slide5_engage.cta: Share your view below and follow for more trade policy updates
